title: How do I inserted soul
wikitext:
[]: How do I inserted soul? Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 08, 2009 10:46 pm Wasting time on the EvaGeeks wiki lead me to this , which reminded me of the one thing that just always completely alluded me: How exactly does NERV get human souls inserted into their EVAs? Yui and 01 is obvious, due to the direct interaction and everything. 02 is kind of fuzzy since Asuka's mom was a suicide, and everything past that is just floating in WTF territory to me. So what does NERV do each time a new unit is commissioned and pilot selection is necessary? Do they just conveniently have everyone's dead mother's consciousness stored on to a hard drive, or do they put on their robe and wizard hat and hold a seance around the feet of the new Eva, or what? Or if the soul just sort of relocates itself inside the Eva on its own, how do they know whose kid to look for? This isn't meant to be a "Which EVA has Whose Soul" thread. That's been done over enough. I just want to understand the general physics here.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:27 pm Someone needs to photoshop a pic of the Ghostbusters and insert Gendo's, Fuyu's, Ritsuko's, and... someone else's head onto their bodies. Honestly, beyond the contact experiments there's simply NO information on this. They obviously have some way of getting them since Rei was killed a couple of times and they still managed to get her soul back into another body.

[]: Post by Merridian » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:43 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Honestly, beyond the contact experiments there's simply NO information on this.

[]: Post by TheLobe » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:49 pm didn't Asuka's mum complete the contact experiment before she died? Thats why she went insane. Maybe she even lost part of her soul.

[]: Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:58 pm Apparently only the maternal side of Kyoko was transferred into Unit-02. That's why she wasn't able to recognize Asuka afterwards, because the part of her that was a mother was no longer there. Then after she committed suicide, the rest of her soul was placed into Unit-02. So according to that, the Eva doesn't even need a complete soul to function.

[]: Post by Rock-Slash » Thu Jul 09, 2009 12:47 am Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: Then after she committed suicide, the rest of her soul was placed into Unit-02. So according to that, the Eva doesn't even need a complete soul to function.

[]: Post by Timstuff » Thu Jul 09, 2009 1:03 am The most practical solution to me seems like NERV probably has all of the pilots' dead moms in a freezer somewhere, and whenever a new pilot is chosen they somehow re-create some part of the contact experiment which allows the mother to be absorbed into the Evangelion. That's just my silly fanwanking at work, though. ;)

[]: Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Thu Jul 09, 2009 1:41 am Rock-Slash wrote: Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: Then after she committed suicide, the rest of her soul was placed into Unit-02. So according to that, the Eva doesn't even need a complete soul to function.

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 09, 2009 3:36 am Timstuff wrote: That's just my silly fanwanking at work, though. ;)

[]: Post by FallenTabris » Thu Jul 09, 2009 3:55 am Rock-Slash wrote: Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: Then after she committed suicide, the rest of her soul was placed into Unit-02. So according to that, the Eva doesn't even need a complete soul to function.

[]: Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Thu Jul 09, 2009 9:01 am Rock-Slash wrote: Fast Tony DeNiro wrote: Then after she committed suicide, the rest of her soul was placed into Unit-02. So according to that, the Eva doesn't even need a complete soul to function.

[]: Post by Axell » Thu Jul 09, 2009 12:44 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Someone needs to photoshop a pic of the Ghostbusters and insert Gendo's, Fuyu's, Ritsuko's, and... someone else's head onto their bodies. Honestly, beyond the contact experiments there's simply NO information on this. They obviously have some way of getting them since Rei was killed a couple of times and they still managed to get her soul back into another body.

[]: Post by Timstuff » Thu Jul 09, 2009 12:55 pm [URL=http://img199.imageshack.us/i/lilithstaypuft.jpg/] [/URL]

[]: Post by Squirrel Ninja » Thu Jul 09, 2009 1:37 pm Not bad Timstuff

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 09, 2009 1:55 pm To answer the questions seriously while enjoying shoops: It's demonstrated that the soul, in the Evaverse, is something that can be objectively, scientifically studied, referred to as the field of Metaphysical Biology or something. And since our bodies are maintained by AT Fields, which come from the soul, it's pretty much a fact that the soul remains behind even after death. Soul extraction technology exists, so when a soul needs to be moved, they can just pull it out of the corpse and stick it in the Eva. As for how the completeness of a soul can be measured: The same way the presence and identity of a soul is.

[]: Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Jul 09, 2009 2:18 pm

[]: Post by Ornette » Thu Jul 09, 2009 2:20 pm Old subject, many existing threads: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6569 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3443 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1156 (old) There's probably more discussion about the contact experiments in the ANF archive, but that's a lot harder to search through because the mods at ANF never did anything about going off-topic back then. And since this thread seems to have veered off to retarded shoops, it's toast.
